title: Swift 6.4 Brings New Language Features and Swift Testing/XCTest Interop
gist: Swift 6.4 (beta in Xcode 27) ships language refinements—async defer, weak let, ~Sendable, anyAppleOS cross-platform availability—plus up to 4x faster URL parsing, C-export via @C, and a new XCTest-to-Swift-Testing bridge that simplifies incremental migration for large test suites.

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 introduces weak let, allowing a class with a weak var property to be declared as Sendable without resorting to @unchecked Sendable, reducing unsafe boilerplate in concurrent codebases.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Reduces @unchecked Sendable workarounds that mask concurrency bugs; watch for adoption in framework SDKs.
insights.locator: At the language level, the new release

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 adds ~Sendable to let developers explicitly opt types out of Sendable conformance, complementing weak let to give finer-grained control over Swift's concurrency type system.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Finer-grained Sendable control reduces false-safe concurrency annotations across Swift libraries.

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 allows defer statements to execute async tasks using await, enabling clean-up code to properly await async operations before a scope exits.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Fills a long-standing async-cleanup gap; simplifies resource management in server-side and concurrency-heavy Swift code.
insights.quote: defer statements support executing async tasks using await
insights.locator: defer statements support executing async tasks

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 generates warnings for Task closures where thrown errors are not handled, tightening error-handling discipline in async code and reducing silent failure risks.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Progressive strictness signals Apple mandating safer async patterns; third-party libraries may need update cycles to maintain compatibility.

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 introduces anyAppleOS, a new platform identifier that unifies iOS, macOS, visionOS, and tvOS availability checks into a single declaration, reducing repetitive boilerplate for multi-platform Swift code.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Signals Apple consolidating its platforms under a single code umbrella; watch for ecosystem tools following suit.
insights.locator: The new anyAppleOS identifier can be used

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 adds @diagnose, a per-function attribute that lets developers silence deprecation warnings, promote warnings to errors, or downgrade future errors to warnings, providing fine-grained compiler control without resorting to global suppression flags.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Incremental migration control for large codebases adopting new Swift versions without breaking builds.

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 bridges XCTest and Swift Testing so XCTest assertion failures appear as Swift Testing issues and Swift Testing APIs can be called from within XCTest, enabling incremental migration without rewriting entire test suites.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Removes the all-or-nothing migration barrier; large iOS/macOS codebases can adopt Swift Testing incrementally while preserving test coverage.
insights.locator: The new Swift release also improves interoperability between XCTest and Swift Testing

insights.claim: Swift 6.4's Test.cancel API allows dynamic cancellation of tests including individual parameterized cases, and a configurable retry limit lets flaky tests repeat until they pass—reducing CI flakiness noise.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Reduces CI flakiness overhead for teams running large Swift test suites in continuous integration.
insights.locator: dynamically cancel tests, including parameterized cases

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 delivers up to 4x faster URL parsing by replacing legacy Objective-C Foundation code with a unified Swift implementation while keeping APIs unchanged.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: URL-heavy apps (networking, deep-link parsing) get a free performance boost after upgrading; validates Apple's ongoing Objective-C-to-Swift Foundation migration strategy.

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 introduces the @C attribute to export Swift functions directly to C, similar to how @objc exposes Swift to Objective-C, with compiler enforcement preventing incompatible C types from being passed.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Opens Swift to system-level and embedded programming use cases where C ABI compatibility is required, expanding Swift's addressable developer base.
insights.locator: expose functions written in Swift to C using the new @C attribute

insights.claim: Swift 6.4 introduces ProgressManager, a new type for structured type-safe progress reporting that integrates with async/await and metadata handling, replacing ad-hoc progress patterns.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Standardizes async progress reporting across Apple frameworks; third-party libraries adding async/await support will benefit from a common API surface.
insights.locator: the release introduces ProgressManager a new type
